_comment: Starter pool for ChallengeService (spec 5.3). Selected deterministically by date seed, avoiding recent repeats. tags drive combo secrets and variety. Keep each doable in <2min, no equipment, no spending, warm/absurd, second-person imperative. Curator's voice.

challenges.text: Photograph three triangular things. They know what they did.
Compliment an inanimate object out loud. Mean it.
Walk somewhere you've never walked. Ten metres counts.
Name a cloud. Introduce yourself. Keep it professional.
Send someone a single, unexplained emoji. Do not follow up.
Draw your current mood as one wobbly line. No lifting the pen.
Find the oldest thing within arm's reach. Thank it for its service.
Stand on one leg until you get bored. Report the boredom to no one.
Invent a word for the exact feeling you have right now. Use it once.
Leave one thing five percent tidier than you found it. Just one.
Eavesdrop on a bird for thirty seconds. Do not translate.
Reorganise something by colour, for no reason at all.
Look out a window and file a one-sentence weather report to yourself.
Take one sip of a drink as slowly as is socially acceptable.
Walk ten steps backwards somewhere safe. Nod like it was intentional.
Tell someone about a song they'd like. No context, just the song.
Find a face in something that isn't a face. Say hello to it.
Stretch the way a cat would if a cat had your exact day.
Write one true sentence about today. It can be very small.
Do one familiar thing in the opposite order. Notice what breaks.
Collect one interesting sound. Keep it in your head like a marble.
Do the smallest kind thing you can get away with unnoticed.
Doodle a creature that does not exist. Give it one worry.
Touch something that grows. A weed in the pavement is perfect.
Give a room in your home a grander name. Use it for one day.
Count something no one has ever needed counted. Remember the number.
Sit up like someone you admire just walked in. Hold it for a breath.
Thank a tool that helped you today. Yes, out loud. Yes, really.
Learn one new word and mispronounce it confidently to yourself.
Find your shadow. Assess its posture. Judge gently.
Leave a kind note somewhere only future-you will find it.
Hum a dramatic theme for a mundane task. Commit fully.
Notice one smell you'd usually ignore. Decide how you feel about it.
Take a five-second detour toward something that caught your eye.
Praise a plant's work ethic. It's been photosynthesising all day.
Take one breath so deep it feels slightly ridiculous. There it is.
